symbols: angel appearing to the woman alone — God's revelation to the one who will bear the deliverer
reclining woman — surprise and awe at the angelic visitation
beam of light — divine presence breaking into the ordinary world
distant landscape — the land of Israel that Samson will be called to deliver
description: A landscape scene with two figures. At the left, a woman in flowing pink-red drapery reclines on the ground, her upper body bare, looking up in surprise. At the right, a young angelic figure in ornate patterned clothing stands, gesturing toward her. Behind them stretches a dramatic landscape with a river, buildings, and hills under a sky with rays of light breaking through clouds. The palette is warm with golden light illuminating the distant vista.
theological_description: The Annunciation to Manoah's wife (Judges 13:2-25) is one of the great birth announcements in the Old Testament, prefiguring the Annunciation to Mary. The Angel of the Lord appears to the barren wife of Manoah and promises a son who will be a Nazirite from the womb, set apart to begin delivering Israel from the Philistines. The pattern is consistent throughout Scripture: God chooses the barren, the forgotten, the overlooked as vessels of His greatest purposes. The angel's visitation to the woman rather than to her husband subverts patriarchal expectation and establishes a direct relationship between God and the mother of the deliverer. This scene stands in typological relationship with Luke 1:26-38: just as the angel came to an unnamed woman promising a son who would deliver Israel, so Gabriel came to Mary promising the Son who would deliver all humanity.

scripture_refs.reason: The Angel of the Lord appearing to Manoah's barren wife to announce Samson's birth
The birth of Samson and the Spirit of the Lord beginning to stir in him
The Annunciation to Mary — the New Testament fulfillment of the pattern of angelic birth announcements
The promise to barren Sarah — the recurring biblical pattern of God choosing the barren for miraculous births
